description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 14.

LP outside of Combat

Think about Legendary Points as a measure of a legendary character’s basic power. Just as a Fighter might attack or a Warlock cast an eldritch blast, a Legendary Fighter can cut reality apart, and a Legendary Warlock can shoot several beams of overwhelming power effortlessly. This applies to Legendary Resistances, too, meaning a stray Wisdom saving throw outside of combat can easily be resisted by a Legendary Character, needing only the next 6 seconds to recover and be able to do it again.

As long as a character can take a round to recover, they are always capable of legendary feats. That is what separates a powerful 17th-level adventurer from a 20thlevel legend.

Roleplay and Exploration Features. Some classes possess powerful features that allow them to create effects more effective in roleplay and exploration scenarios. Some spellcaster features allow them to expend LP to use certain spells without expending a spell slot, meaning they could always have a dimension door at the ready, or the ability to heal another character to full health, given enough minutes. Certain Mythical Techniques give martials the tools to traverse great distances, fly, or open planar rifts, all with the use of Legendary Points.

During combat, some of these features become much harder to use and are often ignored, yet in other aspects of the game, they redefine the baseline capabilities a Legendary Character possesses. The Hulk needs not take a short rest after each Thunderclap, Goku can always use his Instant Transmission, and Satoru Gojo never finds himself without his impenetrable Infinity.

Infinite Legendary Resistances. The only restriction on Legendary Resistances is having the LP to use them and only using one per round. This means saving throws outside of combat will always be succeeded by Legendary Characters. A Dexterity saving throw to avoid falling into a ravine, a Strength saving throw to push through difficult traveling conditions, a Constitution saving throw to avoid the paralysis of a poison dart trap–these mundane challenges aren’t obstacles for legends.

The limitations of your pitiful race amuse me to no end. My blood bleeds black, my eyes see shadow. Don’t you know? I have transcended even my own kin. I am the greatest Teng to have ever

lived!

– The Jester of the Moonsilver Court

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
